title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Copper Mill developers plan to present new options for a 26-story tower in Davis Square after community opposition.
The proposed project includes 502 units, 126 affordable, but residents criticized the size and design in relation to the area.
A project office will open on March 23 at 235 Elm St, showcasing renderings and information; construction could start next year.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte, 37, from Somerville, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his nephews with Down syndrome.
The Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress helped Ponte's family during challenging times, inspiring his marathon participation.
Ponte runs in memory of his late nephew Sebastian and to support nephew Zachary's future.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: An Iranian drone strike in Kuwait killed six U.S. soldiers and injured Boston firefighter and National Guard Major Mark Garcia.
Major Mark Garcia is recovering in Germany after the attack; Governor Maura Healey discussed his recovery timeline.
Garcia, also with Boston's Engine 50, received praise from the Boston Firefighters IAFF Local 718 union for his service.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lowell murder retrial delayed after lead investigator Sgt. Scott Quigley involved in fatal crash.
Quigley allegedly drove drunk in Woburn accident, complicating the Phan brothers' murder case.
Justice Barry-Smith cites ongoing investigation into Quigley as reason for trial's indefinite delay.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman has a .906 save percentage this season, a significant improvement from last year's .892.
The Bruins are in the second Wild Card spot in the Eastern Conference with a record of 36-22-6 (78 points).
Boston may rely on Swayman due to Joonas Korpisalo's recent struggles, with Korpisalo having an .875 save percentage in his last five games.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police body cam footage shows hookahs and strippers at Patriots' private party at Estella restaurant in January.
Patriots players allegedly partied past 2 a.m. with unauthorized adult entertainment, smoking, and liquor law violations reported.
Estella owner Helder George Brandao received a three-day license suspension for the unauthorized event violations.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Byard, 33, signs a one-year, $9 million deal with the New England Patriots after an All-Pro season.
Byard played a key role in the Bears' record plus-22 turnover differential, leading the NFL with seven interceptions in 2025.
Byard has not missed a game in his 10 NFL seasons, showcasing durability across his stints with the Titans, Eagles, and Bears.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt was resentenced to 14-17 years in prison for the 2011 slaying of a Dorchester teen.
Watt's earlier murder conviction was overturned; he pleaded guilty to manslaughter and other charges, eligible for parole due to time served.
In 2011, Jaivon Blake, 16, was fatally shot, and Kimoni Elliot, 14, was injured in a gang-related shooting in Dorchester.

title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams launches 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack with beers inspired by Boston sports figures.
The variety pack includes unique beers themed around Derrick White, Garrett Crochet, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chára.
The pack is available in stores, with a launch party on April 4 at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Race Director Dave McGillivray to run 54th Boston Marathon with kids Max, Luke, and Elle, and astronaut Sunita Williams.
McGillivray began running the Boston Marathon in 1972; this year's race honors ex-wife Susan Hurley's charity contributions.
Astronaut Sunita Williams joins the Finish Strong Foundation's team; previously ran 2007 marathon on space station treadmill.
